layers.name: Application UI
layers.description: Expo Router screens, reusable React Native components, and Skia-powered visual presentation for Elite Ludo.

layers.name: Game Domain
layers.description: Ludo rules, board state, move generation, AI decisions, and gameplay-specific types.

layers.name: Services & State
layers.description: Zustand stores, Supabase clients, edge functions, hooks, and runtime integrations that coordinate application behavior.

layers.name: Shared Foundation
layers.description: Themes, assets, constants, reusable types, and cross-cutting utilities shared throughout the mobile app.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Supabase SQL migrations and schemas defining persistent game, profile, economy, and matchmaking data.

layers.name: Project Configuration
layers.description: Expo, TypeScript, package, environment, linting, and tool configuration governing builds and development.

layers.name: Product Documentation
layers.description: Elite Ludo product, architecture, testing, release, and onboarding documentation.

layers.name: Agent Knowledge
layers.description: Repository-local coding skills and reference material used by AI development agents.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and product requirements to understand Elite Ludo’s premium mobile-game goals, technology choices, and expected player experience. These documents provide the vocabulary for the implementation that follows.

tour.title: Application Bootstrap
tour.description: The root Expo Router layout initializes global providers, navigation, authentication state, and application-wide behavior. The index screen then decides where the player enters the experience.
tour.languageLesson: Expo Router turns files into routes while layout files compose navigation and providers around descendant screens.

tour.title: Identity and Onboarding
tour.description: Authentication and onboarding screens connect the first-run experience to Supabase identity services. Read these together to see validation, OAuth or phone flows, and the transition into the main application.

tour.title: Ludo Domain Model
tour.description: The game types, board geometry, and rules form the deterministic core of Elite Ludo. They define legal state and moves before UI, networking, or persistence concerns are layered on top.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript discriminated unions and shared interfaces make game-state transitions explicit and safer to evolve.

tour.title: Match Runtime
tour.description: The match screen composes the game store with rule evaluation and player perspective. This is the central orchestration point where user input becomes game-state transitions and rendered feedback.

tour.title: Board Rendering
tour.description: Skia components translate domain state into the board, tokens, dice, and particle effects players see. The split keeps rendering concerns separate from the game rules introduced earlier.
tour.languageLesson: React composition keeps visual primitives reusable, while Skia performs high-performance canvas rendering outside ordinary native views.

tour.title: Realtime Multiplayer
tour.description: Matchmaking and realtime modules connect local game state to Supabase sessions and broadcast events. Follow this path to understand room discovery, synchronization, and remote-player updates.

tour.title: Authoritative Moves
tour.description: Supabase Edge Functions validate and apply dice rolls, token moves, turn skips, and board synchronization on the server side. This boundary protects multiplayer integrity instead of trusting the client.

tour.title: Economy and Purchases
tour.description: Wallet, shop, advertising, and in-app purchase modules implement the game economy. Their server functions enforce rewards and purchases so balances remain authoritative.

tour.title: Database Evolution
tour.description: The ordered SQL migrations define profiles, matches, queues, economy records, row-level security, and realtime policies. They reveal the persistent contracts used by both the mobile client and Edge Functions.
tour.languageLesson: Ordered SQL migrations evolve schema and security policies incrementally; row-level security keeps authorization close to the data.

tour.title: Architecture and Verification
tour.description: Finish with the architecture guide and manual test plan to connect implementation details back to intended system boundaries and release checks. These documents are the practical map for extending the project safely.
